authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(5) — Antelope Lake tributaries (Plumas Co.)
sources.quotedText: Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. 5 trout

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Reservoir layer record for gaz_id 256274, "Antelope Lake", Reservoir, Plumas County, CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":256274,"gaz_name":"Antelope Lake","gaz_featureclass":"Reservoir","fcode":39009,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Plumas"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13424206.7757, 4891980.1504) converts to lon -120.591701, lat 40.178944, matching the sibling water file's (antelope-reservoir.json) recorded coordinate (-120.591031, 40.179283) within ~80m. This lake's coordinate is used as the anchor point for these Antelope Lake tributary mouths (Boulder, Lone Rock, Antelope, and Little Antelope creeks), replacing this file's prior unsupported coordinate (-120.5600, 40.1920), which was ~3.0km away and had no GNIS-backed source.

parameters.note: Applies to tributary streams feeding Antelope Lake (Plumas Co.), including Boulder, Lone Rock, Antelope and Little Antelope creeks.

regulations.humanSummary: Open Saturday preceding Memorial Day through September 30
regulations.verbatimText: Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30.

parameters.counting_rule: 5 trout daily bag limit; this special regulation entry does not specify a distinct possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
